query: What are the key principles of Germany's social market economy?
answer: Germany's social market economy (Soziale Marktwirtschaft) combines free market capitalism with social policies. Key principles include competition policy enforced by the Bundeskartellamt, comprehensive social insurance (health, pension, unemployment, long-term care, accident), and the subsidiarity principle. According to the 2024 Bundesbank Annual Report (p.47), the model has delivered an average GDP growth rate of 2.1% over the past decade while maintaining one of the lowest Gini coefficients in the OECD.

query: What is the current state of broadband coverage in rural Germany?
answer: As of 2024, approximately 82% of rural households in Germany have access to broadband speeds of at least 100 Mbit/s. The federal government's Gigabit Strategy aims to achieve nationwide gigabit coverage by 2030. Fibre-to-the-home (FTTH) penetration in rural areas currently stands at around 15%, significantly below the EU average of 25%. The programme has allocated EUR 12 billion in federal subsidies since 2015, though deployment has been slower than anticipated due to planning permission delays.
